title: OpenAI: Workspace Agents Introduced In ChatGPT To Enable Team-Based Autonomous Workflows
summary: OpenAI: Workspace Agents Introduced In ChatGPT To Enable Team-Based Autonomous Workflows OpenAI: Workspace Agents Introduced In ChatGPT To Enable Team-Based Autonomous Workflows By Amit Chowdhry • Today at 10:14 PM OpenAI: Workspace Agents Introduced In ChatGPT To Enable Team-Based Autonomous W
content: OpenAI: Workspace Agents Introduced In ChatGPT To Enable Team-Based Autonomous Workflows [...] OpenAI: Workspace Agents Introduced In ChatGPT To Enable Team-Based Autonomous Workflows [...] OpenAI: Workspace Agents Introduced In ChatGPT To Enable Team-Based Autonomous Workflows [...] OpenAI announced the introduction of workspace agents in ChatGPT, a new capability that enables teams to create shared AI agents designed to handle complex, multi-step workflows across organizations. The feature marks a shift from individual AI assistance toward collaborative, agent-driven work systems operating within enterprise environments. [...] Workspace agents build on earlier GPT-based tools by enabling persistent, cloud-based execution of tasks that continue even when users are offline. Powered by Codex, these agents can perform a wide range of functions, including generating reports, writing and executing code, managing communications, and coordinating workflows across teams and systems. [...] The new capability [...] designed to address a key limitation in existing [...] lack of shared context across [...] Workspace agents can gather data from connected systems, follow predefined processes, request approvals when necessary [...] and manage handoffs between [...] , enabling more [...] Workspace agents are currently available in research preview for ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans, with free usage available through early May 2026 before transitioning to a credit-based pricing model. [...] The launch reflects a broader evolution in enterprise AI, moving from tools that assist individuals to systems that actively coordinate and execute work across teams. By embedding agents directly into workflows and communication channels, OpenAI is positioning workspace agents as a foundational layer for the next generation of organizational productivity.

title: AI Model Market Splits as OpenAI Doubles Prices and DeepS...
summary: AI Model Market Splits as OpenAI Doubles Prices and DeepS... RSSUpdated 3 hours ago AI Pricing War AI Model Market Splits as OpenAI Doubles Prices and DeepSeek Undercuts In 24 hours, OpenAI doubled GPT‑5.5 pricing while DeepSeek launched V4 at one‑ninth the cost.
content: In 24 hours, OpenAI doubled GPT‑5.5 pricing while DeepSeek launched V4 at one‑ninth the cost. The comfortable middle tier of AI models is vanishing, forcing developers to choose between premium integrated stacks and cheap open‑weight alternatives. Here's what the split means for builders. [...] Within 24 hours in late April 2026, the AI model market split into two distinct economies. On April 23, OpenAI launched GPT‑5.5 at $5/million input tokens and $30/million output tokens— exactly double the GPT‑5.4 rate. The next day, DeepSeek released V4‑Pro at $1.74/million input and $3.48/million output, with a V4‑Flash tier at $0.14/$0.28. [...] V4‑Pro output tokens cost roughly one‑ninth as much as GPT‑5.5 output at list price. The price gap between premium and open‑weight models is now wider than it has been in years, and the comfortable middle tier that most coding agents routed through is thinning out. [...] | Model | Input (per 1M tokens) | Output (per 1M tokens) | Context | | | | | | | OpenAI GPT‑5.5 | $5.00 | $30.00 | 1M tokens | | Anthropic Opus 4.7 | $5.00 | $25.00 | 1M tokens | | DeepSeek V4‑Pro | $1.74 | $3.48 | 1M tokens | | DeepSeek V4‑Flash | $0.14 | $0.28 | 1M tokens | [...] OpenAI's pricing isn't just about tokens — it's about selling an integrated stack. GPT‑5.5 is the centerpiece of a system that includes Codex (with expanded computer use, browser interaction, and longer agentic runs), ChatGPT across Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise tiers, and the API. According to The New Stack, "OpenAI is not selling tokens. It is selling outcomes, and outcomes are now priced accordingly." [...] The cadence matters too: GPT‑5.4 shipped in early March, and GPT‑5.5 followed seven weeks later. That's enterprise procurement strategy, not a benchmark race. Cheaper tiers (GPT‑5.4, GPT‑5.4 mini/nano, Batch, Flex, Priority, cached input) remain on the price list, but the flagship is what coding agents default to. [...] GPT‑5.5 scores 82.on Terminal‑Bench 2.0, up from 75.on GPT‑5.4. OpenAI claims token efficiency offsets the price hike, though no precise effective‑cost figure has been published. [...] There's also a durability question. The New Stack argues the gap won't close on its own in the near term: OpenAI will continue releasing fast and pricing up (integrated product as moat), while DeepSeek will continue releasing open weights and pricing down (commodity infrastructure thesis), per The New Stack. The models in between — Claude Sonnet, Gemini Pro, Mistral Large — will face increasing pressure to pick a side. [...] models in most coding

title: DeepSeek Releases V4 Costs 10x Less Than OpenAI, Runs on Huawei Chips
summary: DeepSeek Releases V4 Costs 10x Less Than OpenAI, Runs on Huawei Chips Chinese AI startup DeepSeek released a preview of DeepSeek-V4 on Friday (April 24), its most significant model since R1 shook global markets 15 months ago. The open-source release introduces 1-million-token context as a standard
content: DeepSeek Releases V4 Costs 10x Less Than OpenAI, Runs on Huawei Chips [...] Chinese AI startup DeepSeek released a preview of DeepSeek-V4 on Friday (April 24), its most significant model since R1 shook global markets 15 months ago. The open-source release introduces 1-million-token context as a standard feature — not a premium tier — and marks the company’s first major model optimized for Huawei’s domestic AI chips rather than Nvidia hardware. [...] V4 comes in two versions: V4-Pro with 1.6 trillion total parameters (49 billion active) and V4-Flash with 284 billion parameters (13 billion active). Both are available now on Hugging Face and through DeepSeek’s API. [...] “DeepSeek-V4 Preview is officially live and open-sourced,” the company announced. “Welcome to the era of cost-effective 1M context length.” [...] DeepSeek claims V4-Pro outperforms all other open-source models in world knowledge [...] , trailing only Google’s Gemini 3.1 Pro. In agentic coding evaluations, V4-Pro beats Claude Sonnet 4.5 and approaches Claude Opus 4.6’s non-thinking mode performance. [...] Pricing: 10x Cheaper Than OpenAI [...] | Model | Output (per 1M tokens) | | | | | DeepSeek V4-Flash | $0.28 | | DeepSeek V4-Pro | $3.48 | | OpenAI GPT-5.5 | $30 | | Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 | $25 | [...] V4-Pro currently costs up to 12x more than V4-Flash due to “constraints in high-end compute capacity.” DeepSeek expects Pro pricing to drop sharply once Huawei scales up Ascend 950 production in the second half of this year. [...] The most significant shift from DeepSeek’s earlier models: V4 is optimized for Huawei’s Ascend AI chips rather than Nvidia hardware. [...] Hours after the preview launched, Huawei confirmed that V4 is fully supported on its Ascend 950-based supernode clusters and that its chips were used for part of V4-Flash’s training. “Through close technical collaboration, the entire Ascend supernode product line now supports the DeepSeek-V4 series models,” Huawei said. [...] DeepSeek trained its earlier V3 and R1 models on Nvidia H800 GPUs. V4’s pivot to domestic chips comes as U.S. export controls continue blocking Chinese developers from purchasing Nvidia’s most advanced processors. [...] shows that top Chinese [...] can now run on Chinese hardware.” [...] V4 is still a preview release. DeepSeek hasn’t announced when the final version will ship. [...] The company’s existing deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner API endpoints will be fully retired after July 24, 2026. Developers should migrate to the explicit deepseek-v4-pro and deepseek-v4-flash model IDs before that date

title: GPT-5.5 Arrives: What ChatGPT Users Need to Know
summary: GPT-5.5 Arrives: What ChatGPT Users Need to Know GPT-5.5 Arrives: What Changes for ChatGPT Users GPT-5.5 is OpenAI’s newest advanced model for ChatGPT, Codex, and the API. It is built for harder work, including coding, research, documents, spreadsheets, and longer tasks that need several steps.
content: GPT-5.5 is OpenAI’s newest advanced model for ChatGPT, Codex, and the API. It is built for harder work, including coding, research, documents, spreadsheets, and longer tasks that need several steps. For ChatGPT users, the main change is better reasoning, stronger tool use, and less need to guide every move.¹ [...] GPT-5.5 is not just a faster answer machine. OpenAI is framing it as a model for more complete work sessions. [...] That means the model is meant to handle tasks with several parts. It can review information, use tools, check its own work, and keep going until the request is finished. OpenAI says GPT-5.5 is designed for work such as coding, online research, data analysis, document creation, spreadsheets, and moving across tools.¹ [...] OpenAI says GPT-5.5 understands what a user is trying to do earlier, asks for less guidance, uses tools more effectively, checks its work, and keeps going longer than earlier models.² [...] In ChatGPT, GPT-5.5 appears through Thinking mode for deeper reasoning. OpenAI’s help page says access and usage limits depend on the plan, and some users may see different options during rollout.³ [...] There is also GPT-5.5 Pro, which OpenAI says is meant for tougher questions and higher-accuracy work.¹ But Pro mode has tradeoffs. OpenAI’s help page notes that some ChatGPT tools, including Apps, Memory, Canvas, and image generation, are not available in Pro mode.³ [...] OpenAI’s API changelog says GPT-5.5 is available in the Chat Completions, Responses, and Batch APIs. GPT-5.5 Pro is available for Responses API requests that need more compute.⁴ [...] OpenAI’s model page lists GPT-5.5 with a 1,050,000-token context window.⁵ In regular terms, that gives developers much more room to work with long documents, large codebases, and large sets of instructions. [...] Codex users get a direct upgrade too. OpenAI says GPT-5.5 is now available in Codex and is recommended for many coding tasks when it appears in the model picker. It is especially useful for implementation, refactors, debugging, testing, validation, and knowledge-work materials.⁶ [...] GPT-5.5 shows where ChatGPT is heading. The focus is moving from quick replies to longer tasks that need context, care, and follow-through. [...] 1. OpenAI.“Introducing GPT-5.5.” OpenAI, 23 Apr. 2026. [...] 2. OpenAI.“GPT-5.5 System Card.” OpenAI, 24 Apr. 2026. [...] 3. OpenAI Help Center.“GPT-5.3 and GPT-5.5 in ChatGPT.” OpenAI Help Center, 24 Apr. 2026. [...] 4. OpenAI Developers.“Changelog.” OpenAI API Docs, 24 Apr. 2026. [...] 5. OpenAI Developers.“GPT-5.5 Model.” OpenAI API Docs, 24 Apr. 2026.

title: 斯坦福伯克利英伟达联合发布Agent验证框架：扩展验证计算量超越GPT-5.5
summary: 斯坦福伯克利英伟达联合发布Agent验证框架：扩展验证计算量超越GPT-5.5 - 鲸林向海 斯坦福伯克利英伟达联合发布Agent验证框架：扩展验证计算量超越GPT-5.5 17小时前 • AI产业动态• 阅读 71 本项目由斯坦福大学计算机科学博士生 Jacky Kwok 主导，核心贡献者包括伯克利 EECS 博士生 Shulu Li。通讯作者阵容包括：Ion Stoica（加州大学伯克利分校教授、Databricks 创始人）、Azalia Mirhoseini（斯坦福大学教授，曾任职于 DeepMind 与 Anthropic）以及 Marco Pavone（英伟达 AI 与自
content: 斯坦福伯克利英伟达联合发布Agent验证框架：扩展验证计算量超越GPT-5.5 - 鲸林向海 [...] 斯坦福伯克利英伟达联合发布Agent验证框架：扩展验证计算量超越GPT-5.5 [...] 斯坦福、伯克利与英伟达联合推出了名为 LLM-as-a-Verifier的智能体验证框架。该方案作为一种通用的验证机制，能够与任意 Agent Harness 及模型无缝结合。 [...] 研究显示，通过扩展验证阶段的计算量（scaling verification compute），可显著提升智能体的整体性能。在最具影响力的 AI 编程基准测试 Terminal-Bench上，其表现甚至超越了 GPT-5.5 和 Claude Mythos！ [...] LLM-as-a-Verifier 在 AI 编程基准测试 Terminal-Bench 和 SWE-Bench Verified 中均取得了当前最优（ SOTA）成绩。Transformer 论文作者 Lukasz Kaiser 以及 GAN 作者 Bing Xu 也对该工作进行了转发与关注。 [...] 1. 在 Terminal-Bench 2.0 和 SWE-Bench Verified 等复杂的长时序基准任务中，LLM-as-a-Verifier 的表现全面超越了前沿模型，并均取得了当前最优（SOTA）性能。所有实验结果均来源于官方排行榜。 [...] 1. LLM-as-a-Verifier 能够在不同的 Agent Harness 框架中实现无缝集成，其通用性验证于以下三个基准任务： 2. ForgeCode：验证准确率提升至 86.3. Terminus-Kira：准确率提升至 79.4. Terminus 2：准确率增加至 71.[...] 1. LLM-as-a-Verifier 在验证准确率和消除平局方面全面领先于传统的 LLM-as-a-Judge。即使在增加重复验证次数的情况下（如 k = 16），Verifier 方法依然保持了至少 的验证准确率优势。此外，它完全消除了平局现象。 [...] 从而更接近真实奖励 [...] 「LLM-as-a-Verifier」是一种通用验证机制，能够显著提升智能体的整体性能，并在多个 AI 编程基准上取得当前最优（SOTA）表现，超越了其他前沿模型如 Claude Mythos。 [...] 相比传统的「LLM-as-a-Judge」方法，该框架利用更细致的评分粒度、重复验证以及评估标准分解，实现了更高的验证准确率和更精确的区分能力，彻底消除了评分平局现象。 [...] 实验数据证实，该框架具备良好的通用性，可兼容多种Agent Harness与模型架构，在多项基准任务中显著提升准确率。同时，通过细化评分机制，有效减轻了量化误差，使得验证结果更贴近真实的奖励信号。

title: Claude Managed Agents Get Persistent Memory in Public Bet...
summary: Claude Managed Agents Get Persistent Memory in Public Bet... RSSUpdated 3 hours ago Claude Agents Memory Claude Managed Agents Get Persistent Memory in Public Beta Anthropic has launched persistent memory for Claude Managed Agents in public beta, enabling AI agents to learn across sessions.
content: Claude Managed Agents Get Persistent Memory in Public Beta [...] Anthropic has launched persistent memory for Claude Managed Agents in public beta, enabling AI agents to learn across sessions. Early adopters like Rakuten report fewer errors and lower costs. Here's how the filesystem‑based memory layer works and what it means for builders. [...] Anthropic has launched persistent memory for Claude Managed Agents in public beta, giving AI agents the ability to retain and apply knowledge across sessions. Announced by Angela Jiang, Head of Product for the Claude Platform, the feature addresses a fundamental limitation: until now, every new agent session started from zero, with no memory of past decisions, mistakes, or learned context. [...] As Angela Jiang, Head of Product for the Claude Platform, stated on LinkedIn: "Managed agents can now self learn across sessions with memory — developers can view, edit, add and delete those memories directly in the console or API." [...] Anthropic notes that its Opus 4.7 model is specifically optimized for filesystem‑based memory. According to SD Times, Opus 4.7 "remembers important notes across long, multi‑session work and uses them to move on to new tasks that need less up‑front context." The model is more selective about what to retain and saves more thorough, organized memories compared to earlier versions. [...] Anthropic brought four companies into the early access program, and the results are striking — especially Rakuten's: [...] | Netflix | Carrying context across sessions; mid‑conversation human corrections | Replaces need to manually update prompts [...] | Rakuten | Long‑running task agents avoiding past mistakes | 9 [...] | Wisedocs | Document verification pipeline with cross‑session memory | faster verification | | Ando | Making sense of messy team‑agent conversations | Eliminated need to build custom memory infrastructure | [...] - Deploy via the Claude Console or the new CLI - Documentation: platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed‑agents/memory - Blog announcement: claude.com/blog/claude‑managed‑agents‑memory [...] The memory feature is included in the Managed Agents public beta — no additional access request required beyond the base product. [...] The competitive landscape is shifting fast. OpenAI's Codex and workspace agents are pushing in the same direction. Google's Project Mariner is building agent infrastructure with its own memory layer. But Anthropic is the first to ship persistent memory as a first‑class, filesystem‑based product with real enterprise adoption numbers behind it.
